title: Spicy Juicy Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A hearty Tunisian chicken tagine that blends fiery spices, fragrant herbs, and a crunchy graham cracker crust for a unique twist. Lowcarb and diabeticfriendly, its perfect for meal prep.

ingredients.name: Spice blend (ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, harissa paste, ground cinnamon)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Pat the chicken dry with paper towels.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat. Brown the chicken pieces 3–4 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.
Reduce the heat to medium. Add the sliced onion and bell pepper; cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the spice blend, tomato paste, and Dijon mustard; cook 1 minute to release aromas.
Return the chicken to the pot. Pour in the chicken broth, add lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover the pot, transfer to the oven, and braise for 25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
While the tagine finishes, toast the graham cracker crumbs in a small skillet over medium heat until golden, about 3–4 minutes. Set aside.
Remove the pot from the oven, let rest 5 minutes. Sprinkle the toasted graham cracker crumbs over the chicken and gently stir to incorporate.
Serve hot, optionally topping each portion with a dollop of Greek yogurt and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro.
